metrics.narrative: The buyer's stated business problem is a technology gap for managing long-term contracts (Customer_Business_Problem__c), driven by growth in managed contracts from 4-5 per year to over 25, with missed obligations on high-value energy contracts carrying financial-penalty exposure (Compelling_Event__c). The 29 Apr 2026 demo reinforced this: the buyer's team confirmed missing obligations 'poses breach risk and potential financial penalties' and that automated alerts and calendar sync 'aim to reduce manual oversight and ensure compliance.' No formal ROI or cost-of-inaction dollar figure has been quantified beyond this qualitative breach-penalty framing, so the business case is real but not fully quantified.

champion.narrative: Luke Davies, an Operations Analyst, is the sole consistently active buyer-side contact and has twice voiced a stated Zycus preference (13 Jan 2026: 'positive movement and likely to Zycus'; 16 Mar 2026: 'preference is for Zycus'), and he replied again on 2 Jul 2026 to confirm the evaluation is still active. However his title is junior relative to the Deputy General Counsel and IT Manager who also attended the demo, there is no evidence he has advocated for Zycus with senior stakeholders, and the rep himself noted being 'concerned about the lack of engagement with us' on 12 Jun 2026. This reads as a developing, cooling champion rather than a confirmed one.

competition.narrative: Ironclad is the named competitor (Competitors__c) and confirmed co-finalist: the buyer's PM was 'told to issue RFP to both shortlisted vendors' around 25 Mar 2026. Zycus's own team acknowledged a real capability gap versus the field on 13 Apr 2026 ('we have gaps - we do not have detailed obligation and task management'), and is countering with a 12-month roadmap commitment for an obligation dashboard and rule-based escalations demoed on 29 Apr 2026. The buyer's status quo is manual tracking via the relevant stakeholder and EnergyOne, a genuine 'do nothing' alternative given the deal's historically low urgency.

identify_pain.narrative: The buyer's current process is manual: 'Tracking obligations manually, using spreadsheets and emails to relate incoming data to obligations' (29 Apr 2026 call, Current Workflow), against systems named as Business Central and EnergyOne (How_are_you_addressing_your_problem_toda__c). The buyer confirmed a specific pain: 'no automated escalation workflows beyond basic notifications, requiring manual follow-up when tasks fall behind,' directly tied to the compelling event of growing contract volume and breach-penalty exposure.

paper_process.narrative: Zycus submitted final commercials and offered a Letter of Intent around 16 Mar 2026 ('final commercials submitted - preference is for Zycus and have offered LOI'), but no MSA, SOW, or other contracting paper has since been discussed or logged. The deal remains at Shortlisted, pre-selection, so a formal paper process has not started.

economic_buyer.narrative: Executive_Sponsor_Identified__c is false and X10a_Sponsor__c is explicitly 'No'; Who_will_approve_budget__c is blank. Nick Middleton, Deputy General Counsel & Head of Contract Management, attended the 29 Apr 2026 demo and is the most plausible internal approver by seniority, but he has not been engaged one-on-one and no field or call names him as budget holder. On a $110,000 deal with a repeatedly slipping timeline, the absence of any confirmed economic buyer is a structural gap.

decision_process.narrative: The buyer ran a formal RFP, issuing it to 'both shortlisted vendors' (Zycus and Ironclad) around 25 Mar 2026 per the rep's Next Step notes, with evaluation involving IT, contract management, and compliance representatives per the 29 Apr 2026 call. The process has stalled repeatedly: IT delayed the selection process by '2-3 weeks' on 9 Jun 2026, and as of 2 Jul 2026 the buyer was still 'finishing our review of the original requirements' with no confirmed decision date — a real but unreliable process.

decision_criteria.narrative: Business_Requirements__c is blank and Shoe_Fit_Criteria_Met__c is false in Salesforce, but the 29 Apr 2026 demo surfaced concrete evaluation criteria directly from the buyer: end-to-end obligation tracking with automated alerts and a personal task dashboard, contract variation workflows with version history and approval logic, and supplier performance data ingestion linked to obligations. These criteria are real and specific but have not been formally logged or scored against Zycus in the CRM.

deal_risk.text: Five consecutive close-date pushes (25 Mar → 31 Aug 2026) and a 63% amount reduction ($300,000 → $110,000) reflect a deal that keeps re-scoping and re-dating itself rather than progressing toward a decision.

The 10 Feb 2026 Next Step records this as a secondary priority behind '1 higher-priority project in motion' with no budget owner ever named, creating real risk of indefinite deprioritization even with a Zycus-preferring buyer.

win_position.text: The buyer twice voiced an explicit preference for Zycus over Ironclad (13 Jan 2026: 'positive movement and likely to Zycus'; 16 Mar 2026: 'final commercials submitted, preference is for Zycus,' with an LOI offered), and a 21 May 2026 written buyer update confirmed 'Zycus remain a shortlisted vendor.'

No economic buyer or budget approver has ever been named (X10a_Sponsor__c = 'No'; Executive_Sponsor_Identified__c = false) on this $110,000 deal, and the stated buyer preference is now over three months old with no reaffirmation since.

Zycus's own team acknowledged a capability gap in detailed obligation and task management (13 Apr 2026) directly relevant to the RFP comparison against Ironclad, countered by a 12-month roadmap commitment that is not yet formally documented in writing.

deal_momentum.text: The buyer replied directly to the deal owner's check-in on 2 Jul 2026 (7 days ago), confirming the evaluation is still active and that their PM will follow up on next steps.

This is the fifth time since March 2026 that a promised re-engagement or decision date has slipped (25 Mar, 11 May, 25 May, 12 Jun, 2/6 Jul 2026) without a confirmed follow-through, and the deal has sat in Shortlisted for roughly 177 days against an expected ~18-day cadence.

customer_commitment.text: The buyer submitted an LOI and final commercials with a stated Zycus preference on 16 Mar 2026, and dedicated a multi-person team (IT, contract management, compliance) to a detailed scenario-based demo on 29 Apr 2026.

Committed feedback on the 29 Apr 2026 demo (promised for 1 May 2026) was never delivered on schedule; instead the buyer cited a further internal requirements review that has now continued for over two months.

hard.ais_why:
• Per the 11 May 2026 rep note: 'Delayed a few weeks due to additional requirements being considered, mainly on ERP integration. They are sending questions this week and we will schedule an integration session.' On 13 Apr 2026: 'Working through RFP and we have some gaps in obligation tracking but they still want Zycus to respond and to see demo of what we can do now and understand what we will commit to adding, within 12 months.' Buyer concerns are ERP integration and obligation tracking ‚Äî pure traditional CLM.
• On 26 Feb 2026: 'Running another demo on their use case, high value energy contracts.' The deal is anchored in high-value energy contracts (CLM use case) ‚Äî not AI capability. Merlin and Merlin Intake appear in product scope but never in dated rep dialogue, RFP commentary, or demo focus across the 2,380-char NextStep.
• Confidence is Medium because the NextStep is moderately rich but covers a discrete CLM evaluation rather than a broad S2P. The absence of any AI keyword (AI, Merlin, agentic, GenAI) across the entire history despite Merlin/Merlin Intake bundling in Products suggests Zycus has positioned AI optionally; buyer is not pulling for it.
